Empires & Puzzles Norman Review: The Jousting Champion of Bypass
Meet Norman, the Champions family hero who treats Dodge and Taunt like mere suggestions. Our deep dive analyzes if his unique bypass mechanics can still pierce the armor of the modern meta.
Norman Review: The Knight Who Said 'No' To Taunt
Norman, The Jousting Champion: Unstoppable Charge Through Taunt and Dodge
Norman is a 5-star Fire (Red) hero from the

family, originally released in January 2024. He arrived as a direct answer to the frustration of "Can't Touch This" defenses—specifically targeting the Dodge and Taunt meta that was rampant at the time. With a lance bigger than his horse and a grin that says "I didn't hear no bell," Norman is an Average speed fighter designed to ensure your team's hits actually land. While he doesn't have a costume yet, his base kit is specialized enough to warrant a look even years later. He's the guy you bring when you're tired of seeing "Dodge" pop up fifteen times in a row.

Norman looks exactly like what you'd expect from a "Jousting Champion"—he's a classic medieval knight, but with a slightly exaggerated, almost caricature-like quality. He’s got the full plate armor, the plumed helmet, and a lance that looks like a candy cane made of steel. His expression is one of smug confidence, like he just won the tournament and stole your lunch money. It's a charmingly traditional design in a game filled with eldritch horrors and steampunk constructs.

When Norman was released, his stats (1013 Attack / 992 Defense / 1685 HP) were considered solid. However, we are now in an era where new heroes routinely push 1300+ Attack and 2200+ HP out of the box.
. He needs emblems and Limit Breaks to threaten modern health pools.
. He can be squishy without his LB2 power.
. Modern snipers can delete this amount of health very quickly.
Norman's stats have aged significantly. To use him effectively in the modern meta, you absolutely must commit to Limit Breaking him (LB2 strongly recommended) to bring his survivability up to par.
The "Unstoppable Charge" is a fascinating mix of selfish power and team support.
Norman himself will smack Constance, Sha Wujing, or any Rogue right in the face. He does not care about their evasion tricks.

Dealing 320% damage to all enemies at Average speed is decent on paper, but given his older Attack stat, it's more of a "softener" than a team-wiper against modern defenses.
If he gets hit with a status ailment (very common), his mana generation jumps. With a Level 29 Magic/Styx troop, he can effectively become Fast or even Very Fast in the right conditions.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Champions family bonus boosts Special Skill damage by +30%/+50%/+70% for 1/2/3 heroes. Even solo, Norman gets a +30% boost. This is significant because it's a separate multiplier, helping his older stats punch a bit above their weight class.
Norman's passives are arguably better than his active skill in some matchups:
This makes him incredibly resilient to mana controllers or DOT heavy teams. Instead of being slowed down, he speeds up.
A hard counter to Taunt tanks. They Taunt, he gains mana.
Norman's special skill flies right past the Taunting hero to hit everyone else. This is unique and powerful.

is a top-tier defensive power. At the start of the battle (and every wave), he has a chance to reduce ANY damage to 1. This gives him a "Black Knight" style survivability that can keep him alive long enough to fire, compensating for his lower base Defense stat.
Upon release, Norman was seen as a "nice to have" but not "game-breaking" hero. He was the answer to a Dodge meta that was already starting to shift.
His stats have fallen behind significantly. The 320% damage hit, while respectable in 2024, tickles modern 2026 tanks.
Norman is no longer a plug-and-play hero for defense or general offense. However, he is a
He makes high-level Riposte teams safe to attack.
He is one of the few heroes who can reliably hit through everything to finish off a low-HP backline hidding behind a Phorcys or Ludwig.
Norman does not currently have a costume.
Norman is a Fighter, and fighters revive. This synergizes perfectly with his role as a persistent threat.
Maximizes his AoE damage and makes the most of his family bonus damage boost. He needs every point of Attack he can get to remain relevant.
Slightly squishier, relying heavily on his Revive and Aether Power to survive.

His damage becomes negligible, turning him into a pure support hero.

Punishes enemies for using Ailments or Taunt by gaining mana.

Specifically against teams relying on heavy buffs (Counterattack, Defense Up) or Taunt tanks.
His damage is decent, and his passives punish opponents who try to debuff him.
Great for stages with annoying bosses who spam defense buffs.
Norman strips the safety of defensive buffs/counterattack. Phenexa resets the duration or boosts damage. Gestalt capitalizes on the open season to nuke targets.
If facing a heavy counterattack team (like Cyprian/Obakan tanks in tournaments), Norman allows your heavy hitters (Sobek/El Naddaha) to fire their AoEs without killing themselves on the reflect.
Norman hates heroes who reduce his stats (Wither) because his base stats are already lower.
ailments, but direct mana steal (like from some Goblins or Waterpipe minions) still hurts him.

If you pull Norman as an early game player, he is fantastic. His Fighter class will keep him alive in difficult map stages, and his ability to ignore annoying boss buffs will save you many headaches. Level him up and put him on your defense team; he will serve you well for a long time.

Norman is a tool for your toolbox, not a daily driver. Do not put him on defense; he is too easy to play around. Keep him for War attacks against specific annoying setups (Obakan/Bastet combos). He is worthy of emblems, but think twice before spending Alpha Aethers unless you desperately need a counter to Riposte wars.
When released, there was significant confusion in the community. Players asked,
"Does he let my team bypass Taunt?"
. Only he bypasses Taunt. The team only bypasses defensive
Originally, his damage was lower (around 285%). It was buffed to 320% shortly after release/beta adjustments, which helped him feel less like a "wet noodle."
but noted that Norman's ability to ignore Taunt
Dodge personally made him a more reliable initiator, even if his raw damage ceiling was lower.

Norman is the quintessential "solution" hero. You won't use him every raid, but when you see a team hiding behind three layers of defensive buffs, Dodge, and Taunt, Norman is the key that unlocks the door. He’s aging like a classic car—style and specific performance are still there, even if he can't beat the new models in a drag race.
Costume thoughts: A costume that updates his stats to 2026 standards would instantly propel him back to top-tier status, as his mechanic is timeless.
Worth leveling for depth, but strictly an offensive counter-pick in the modern era.
A comprehensive review of Norman, the 5-star Fire hero from the Champions family. Analyze his unique bypass mechanics, stats, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
